Notice Of Motion To Quash Petitioner And Co-Trustee Barbera Brooks Subpoena For Business Records To Pfp Holding Company Ii, Llc Dba Prime Finance And Request For Attorneys Fees
Off Calendar Jan-25-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Eric Buescher for Barbara Brooks. Kevin Dwight and Stephanie Roeser for Hendrick Jones.

The Court stated that it has read the papers and confirmed that matter started with a petition to remove trustee. Mr. Buescher stated that a petition was filed to remove Henrick and Barbera would step down. The Court asked that times are in the trust. Mr. Buescher stated that there are various financial investments. Mr. Dwight responded that there is cash and about $7 million worth of assets. The Court and counsel had a brief discussion about the family limited partnership of Presidio and Buckhill.

Mr. Dwight presented his oral argument in support of his position that the subpoena for business records was too broad. Mr. Buescher argued that he has discussions with Prime and that he was willing to narrow records that the subpoena is seeking. Furthermore Mr. Buescher argued that Presidio and Buckhill are intertwined, that Mr. Dwight asked his to withdrawal the subpoena, that the documents requested are not related to Hendrick's personal investments and that as co-trustee Barbera is entitled to the documents. Mr. Dwight responded that the subpoena is over broad, prior to filing this motion Barbera was unwilling to narrow down her request of documents and the documents requested do related to Hendrick's personal records.

The Court informed counsel that meet and conder means try to work it out. Both counsel provided case background.

The Court rules as follows. In regards to items 1 - 10, the motion is denied. In regards to items 11 - 14, the motion is granted. Attorney's fees for Mr. Dwight is granted in the amount of $1,000.00. Mr. Dwight to prepare a form of order that approved as to form.

Judge John K. Stewart. Clerk: Nancy Wong. Bailiff: Deputy Malohney. Not Reported, D501.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Mediation
Off Calendar Mar-28-2016 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard together: PTR-15-298684 & PTR-15-298685 The Irrevocable Trusts for Elsabet T. Jones.
Appearances: Erick Buescher for (petitioner) Barbara Brooks. Kevin Dwight for (respondent) Henrik Jones.

Mr. Buescher reported that the agreement has fallen through, that they hope to continue with negotiations and that they request a 90 day continuance on the trial date. The Court stated that counsel will need to request a continuance in department 206. Mr. Dwight stated that he has a different take on the status, that the settlement was a global resolution including the family law matter, that they file a 664, that it was just heard on Friday and that they will take the steps to enforce the settlement agreement. Mr. Buescher stated that they disagree on the merits of 664. The status on mediation was taken off calendar.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Mediation
Off Calendar Feb-29-2016 Continued To Mar-28-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Mediation
Off Calendar Jan-25-2016 Continued To Feb-29-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard together: PTR-15-298684 The Elsabett Jones irrevocable trust and PTR-15-298685 The Elsabett Jones Irrevocable Trust II.
Appearances: Kevin Dwight for Henrik Jones. Eric Buescher for (petitioner) Barbara Brooks. Mather Daetwyler, Guardian ad Litem.

Mr. Dwight reported that following the status hearing the parties reached a settlement agreement, that they are documenting the agreement and that it is a global settlement including the family law matter. The status on mediation (both cases) was continued to February 29, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

Petition For Removal Of Trustee
Off Calendar Oct-26-2015 Continued To May-16-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard together: PTR-15-298684 & PTR-15-298685 In Re the Irrevocable Trusts fbo Elsabet T. Jones

Appearances: Erick Buescher & John Minton for (petitioner) Barbara Brooks. Kevin Dwight for and with (respondent) Henrik Jones. Martha Daetwyler, Guardian ad Litem for Elsabet Jones. Holly Schaitberger, counsel in the Family Law matter also present.

Mr. Buescher reported that they submitted a statement, that they need an additional 120 days, that there is substantial discovery going on, that depositions are set for December and then they can consider where they are. Mr. Dwight stated that they are frustrated, that the case is stalled, that Mr. Jones wants to stay involved, that he has successfully administered the trusts since they were established, that there are substantial gains recognized, that there is a parallel family law proceeding, that this action is a negotiating point, that there is a 3rd trust in which Mr. Jones and Ms. Brooks serve as co-trustees with no issues, that they request an evidentiary hearing or a trial date, that they have been waiting on discovery from Ms. Brooks, that there is alleged non-compliance with court orders, that they are willing to mediate, that there was a productive meeting in the family law matter with Marjorie Slabach, that a global resolution would be ideal and that they are not sure the matters can be resolved individually. Mr. Minton stated that the trust proceedings will have to rise and fall on their own merits, that there is a difficult divorce in progress, that the parties should step aside and allow a neutral trustee to be put in place, that there are financial irregularities, that there are many documents to pour over, that they are working with a forensic expert on it, that there are concerns regarding the movement of funds in and out of the accounts, that 80% of the assets are invested in esoteric investments, that it is hard to access and figure out what has happened, that they feel it is too early to set a trial and that they will be in a better position in 90-120 days. The Court stated that if counsel is not in a position for trial they could get a trial date and then a continuance in Department 206 and asked when mediation could be done. Mr. Minton responded that mediation could be complete by the latter part of January 2016. Mr. Dwight stated that they request that an April trial date be set, that records were produced in May, that they do not anticipate many witnesses, that Mr. Jones has an MBA from Harvard and that the investments that Mr. Minton called "esoteric" are investments that Mr. Jones has worked with throughout his career. Ms. Daetwyler stated that she has met with Elsabet, that Elsabet has no information to add, that she is not taking sides, that she was not aware of the terms of the trust, that when she is 21 years old (she will be 18 in December) she can step in as a co-trustee and that she is looking forward to working with her father. Mr. Minton stated that they would want the trial date set farther out, possibly June or July. The Court set a deadline for mediation and status (both matters) on January 25, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The matters were referred to the department of the Presiding Judge (206) to be set for trial on May 16, 2016.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Watts. (Not Reported)
